releases.description:
Mini-RAG v2.0.1 Release Notes
==============================

## Features
- Change model mid-chat

## Patches
- Support fallback to V1 behavior, when generating embeddings not supported by local model
- Fix embedding model not found error

==============================

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/jjwheatley/mini-rag/compare/2.0.1...2.1.0

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Mini-RAG v2.0.0 Release Notes
==============================

## Improved Search
Mini-RAG now chunks notes and folders into excerpts, converts each chunk into a vector embedding, and uses cosine similarity to select the most relevant excerpts for each query. This means the model receives tighter, more targeted context. This effect of Mini-RAG's new semantic search is most noticable with large notes and folders.

## New Settings
### Dedicated Embedding Model
A new setting, "Use a dedicated embedding model", lets you choose a separate Ollama model for generating embeddings. Dedicated embedding models are smaller, faster, and typically produce better retrieval results. For your convenience, Mini-RAG provides a curated list of models to use along with the commands to install them. The current list is:

  | Embedding Model                  | Size    | Notes                                              |
  |------------------------|---------|----------------------------------------------------|
  | all-minilm             | ~46 MB  | Smallest option; good for lower-end hardware       |
  | nomic-embed-text       | ~274 MB | Solid retrieval quality at a reasonable size; good default |
  | mxbai-embed-large      | ~670 MB | Top MTEB benchmark scores for its size class       |
  | snowflake-arctic-embed | ~670 MB | Tuned specifically for retrieval tasks             |
  | bge-m3                 | ~1.2 GB | Multilingual; use this if your notes aren't in English |

If the selected model is not installed use the one-click copy button to get the ollama pull command.

When the setting is disabled, the chat model is used for embeddings.

### Chat Model (previously "Model")
The model dropdown now loads dynamically from Ollama, with a refresh button and inline error messages if Ollama is unreachable. Changing the Ollama URL automatically refreshes the list.

### Retrieved chunks (top-K): 
controls how many note excerpts are injected into each query (range 1–10, default 3). Increase for broader context; decrease for tighter, faster responses.

## Chat buttons
Mini-RAG now uses icon buttons for a cleaner UI & intuitive feel.

### Save As
Conversations can now be saved under a custom filename. Click the chevron (▾) next to the Save button and choose "Save as…" to pick a name before saving. The plain Save button (disk icon) still quick-saves using the default name.

### Summarize
When a chat is opened from a file or folder, a Summarize button (sparkles icon) appears in the toolbar. Clicking it asks the model to produce a summary of the context content.

## Other Improvements
- Chats can now be opened from the Command Palette (Ctrl/Cmd+P → "Mini-RAG: Open chat panel") in addition to the right-click context menu.
- A more responsive chat; responses stream in token-by-token as the model generates them.
- Clicking any chat bubble (yours or the model's) copies its text to the clipboard.
- General UI polish across the chat window, buttons, and settings panel.
- Better support for Obsidian themes.
==============================

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/jjwheatley/mini-rag/compare/1.0.0...2.0.0

## Description
First release of the Mini-Rag plugin for Obsidian.

## Features
- Context-Sensitive Chats with Local LLMs
- Context-Free Chats with Local LLMs
